explanation_used:
1. **Opening statement**: This neuron strongly responds to **proper nouns accompanied by titles, roles, or extended identifiers within formal or news-reporting contexts** (such as political figures named with titles and affiliations, disaster officials cited with full names and agencies, or individuals referenced in legal or criminal proceedings).

2. **Domain coverage**: The pattern appears predominantly in multilingual news headlines and reportage, spanning English, Spanish, Italian, Indonesian, Hungarian, Polish, Bengali, and others. Topics include political announcements, legal cases, disaster management, and crime reporting, where individuals are formally identified by their full name, title (e.g., "NC president," "minister," "General"), organization, or role in a specific event.

3. **Contrast with low-activation examples**: The high-activation texts consistently contain specific named entities embedded within descriptive, formal phrases (e.g., "Tehreek-e-Hurriyat," "BNPB, Sutopo Purwo Nugroho," "Chief Justice of India Ranjan Gogoi"). Low-activation examples lack this structured naming; they feature generic phrases, cultural/entertainment references without formal titles, or incomplete name contexts (e.g., "Song Kang," "10-year-old girl," "guidance of").

4. **Closing interpretation**: The neuron detects formal referential expressions that anchor a person or organization to a specific real-world event or institutional context. It acts as a filter for densely informative, journalistic-style identifiers across languages, while ignoring casual mentions, incomplete names, or non-newsworthy content.

results.actual_text: two to five years and to make it a non-bailable offense. President Arif Alvi signed the Prevention of Electronic Crimes (Amendment) Ordinance,
results.plain_text: Kamran Chaudhry, Lahore  Updated: February 22, 2022 09:53 AM GMT  Human rights activists have deplored Pakistan amending a law to increase the jail term for online criticism of the state by citizens from two to five years and to make it a non-bailable offense. President Arif Alvi signed the Prevention of Electronic Crimes (Amendment) Ordinance, 2022, on Feb. 20. It imposes stricter penalties for spreading fake news and hate speech on electronic and social media platforms. The amended law will also pave the way for action over hate speech and fake news against national institutions including the armed forces and national figures. Cases will be supervised by a high court and the trial court will have to conclude the case within six months.  Opposition parties have joined journalist unions in opposing the new law. “Not only is this legislation undemocratic but it will also inevitably be used to clamp down on dissenters and critics of the government and state institutions,” stated the Human Rights Commission of Pakistan (HRCP) in a tweet. “All government and state functionaries are reminded that they are accountable to citizens as elected representatives and public servants, respectively. It is their job to heed criticism. With other problematic laws in place to counter defamation, the proposed ordinance must be rolled back immediately.” It's election year, the government has failed to deliver, and silencing critics is the only option for a fascist PM Lawyer and rights activist Jibran Nasir agreed. “This way proxies could be used to file FIRs [first information reports]. All channels, anchors and reporters have been exposed to criminal proceedings. A journalist could be arrested, harassed, confined and punished even before trial before one can ascertain whether a news report was true or false,” he said. “Say goodbye to investigative journalism. It's the election year, the government has failed to deliver, and silencing critics is the only option for a fascist PM.” Kashif Aslam, deputy director of the National Commission for Justice and Peace (NCJP), the Church's human rights body in Pakistan, called it the height of intolerance.  “We had been raising concerns against PECA since it was originally legislated by the former government in 2016. Now our own statements, addressing the state, can be used against us. This law targets freedom of expression. Meanwhile, the country passes through multiple crises including inflation,” he told UCA News. According to a study conducted by media rights watchdog Freedom Network, 23 journalists and information practitioners were either sent notices by the Federal Investigation Agency under PECA or charged with offenses under the same law during the period 2019-21. Three journalists have already died this year in Lahore, Karachi and Jhol town of Sanghar district, Sindh province. Article 19 of Pakistan’s constitution guarantees the right to freedom of speech and the press. However, it is conditional on restrictions imposed by the law in the interests of Islam or the security of Pakistan, friendly relations with foreign states, public order, decency or morality, or in relation to contempt of court or incitement to an offense.  ….As we enter the first months of 2022, we are asking readers like you to help us keep UCA News free.  For the last 40 years, UCA News has remained the most trusted and independent Catholic news and information service from Asia. Every week, we publish nearly 100 news reports, feature stories, commentaries, podcasts and video broadcasts that are exclusive and in-depth, and developed from a view of the world and the Church through informed Catholic eyes.  Our journalistic standards are as high as any in the quality press; our focus is particularly on a fast-growing part of the world - Asia - where, in some countries the Church is growing faster than pastoral resources can respond to – South Korea, Vietnam and India to name just three.  And UCA News has the advantage of having in its ranks local reporters who cover 23 countries in south, southeast, and east Asia. We report the stories of local people and their experiences in a way that Western news outlets simply don’t have the resources to reach. And we report on the emerging life of new Churches in old lands where being a Catholic can at times be very dangerous.  With dwindling support from funding partners in Europe and the USA, we need to call on the support of those who benefit from our work.

predictions.text: Kashif Aslam, deputy director of the National Commission for Justice and Peace (NCJP), the Church's human rights body in Pakistan, called it the height of intolerance.
predictions.reasoning: This section includes a proper noun with an extended title and organizational affiliation in a formal news context.

results.actual_text: . Alper'in varlıklı ve akademisyen babası Eşref rolü ile sevilen dizinin kadrosuna giren Altan Er
results.plain_text:
ATV'nin sevilen dizisi Bir Küçük Gün Işığı 20. bölüm fragmanı yayınlandı. Sosyal medyada büyük ses getiren fragmana "O evde herkes bir şey saklıyor" sözleri damga vurdu.  ALTAN ERKEKLİ BİR KÜÇÜK GÜN IŞIĞI'NDA! 
Pazartesi akşamları atv ekranlarında yayınlanan, yapımını NGM'nin, yapımcılığını Nazlı Heptürk'ün üstlendiği, sevilen dizi "Bir Küçük Gün Işığı"na deneyimli isim Altan Erkekli katıldı. Alper'in varlıklı ve akademisyen babası Eşref rolü ile sevilen dizinin kadrosuna giren Altan Erkekli'nin setten ilk kareleri de basınla paylaşıldı. Kendine has üslubu ve renkli karakteri ile dikkat çeken Eşref'in gelişi dizide yaşanacak gelişmeler şimdiden merak konusu oldu.  Yürekleri ısıtan anlatımı ve kalplere dokunan etkileyici hikâyesi ile "Bir Küçük Gün Işığı" her pazartesi atv'de.  Başrollerini Seray Kaya, Berk Oktay, Esra Dermancıoğlu, Tuğçe Açıkgöz, Şeyma Korkmaz, Azra Aksu, Güneş Hayat, Yılmaz Bayraktar, Beran Kotan, Gizem Ünsal ve Yiğit Yapıcı'nın paylaştığı dizinin senaryosunu Özgür Evren Heptürk kaleme alırken, yönetmenliğini ise Emre Kabakuşak üstleniyor.

results.plain_text: Home » News » Gov. Shettima orders probe of alleged child prostitution, sodomy, abortion in Borno prison News Gov. Shettima orders probe of alleged child prostitution, sodomy, abortion in Borno prison Published on March 23, 2019 By Wale Odunsi The Borno State Governor, Kashim Shettima will on Monday inaugurate an investigative panel over a report alleging incidents of child prostitution, sodomy and forced abortion at the Maiduguri maximum security prison. The panel is to be chaired by the state’s Attorney General and Commissioner of Justice, Barrister Kaka Shehu Lawan, while the Permanent Secretary, Ministry of Women Affairs and Social Development, is to serve as Secretary. Shettima, on Saturday, tweeted: “I am immediately setting up a committee under the chairmanship of the Hon. Attorney General and Commissioner of Justice alongside some key officials to visit the prisons; meet officials, investigate and report to me as soon as possible”. Members of the Committee are: Barr. K.S.Lawan, Attorney General; Hauwa Mai-Musa, Permanent Secretary, Women Affairs Ministry; Barr. Mohammed Umaru, Chairman, NBA Maiduguri and Barr. Amina Mustafa Ibrahim, Chairperson, Federation of International Women Lawyers, FIDA. Others are: representative of the Nigeria Police, representative of the Department of State Services, representative of the Comptroller, Nigeria Prisons Service, Borno, Jummai Usman Mshelia, State Coordinator, National Human Rights Commission. Subscribe to Daily Post. Advertise with us. Send us enquiries, press releases. Related Topics:AbortionbornoprostitutionShettimaSodomy You may like Group decries teenage prostitution in Delta Abia: Sex workers go into hiding in Umuahia as govt declares war on prostitution Pay salaries, pension – NLC tasks Shettima Boko Haram: Troops foil attack on military base in Borno Nigeria Decides: PDP rejects Borno guber election result Nigeria Decides: INEC announces winner of Borno guber election

results.actual_text: El coche de la víctima chocó contra varios vehículos Cuando el detenido conf
results.plain_text: El coche de la víctima chocó contra varios vehículos Cuando el detenido confesaba el crimen a la policía, su hermana todavía estaba viva. Emergencias recibió varias llamadas a primera hora alertando de que se habían escuchado varias detonaciones y que había una mujer herida en la calle en el barrio de Almatriche. Inmediatamente fue trasladada al Doctor Negrín.  La mujer de 42 años llegó en parada cardiaca a Urgencias del Negrín minutos antes de las ocho de la mañana. Los sanitarios lograron recuperarla y sobre la marcha pasó a quirófano. A pesar del trabajo de los médicos, falleció en la mesa de operaciones porque los tres disparos recibidos afectaron a órganos vitales. El crimen ha conmocionado a los vecinos de la víctima y a lo habitantes de Jinámar donde murió la otra persona, un hombre hermano de la mujer. La familia de los protagonistas de esta tragedia está destrozada.  Son familiares de las víctimas, lloran desconsolados por la pérdida de José y María Noelia. Están en el Instituto anatómico forense, donde se realizaran las autopsias.  La policía investiga si origen de este doble crimen está relacionado con problemas en la empresa familiar, esta fábrica de productos de limpieza, de la que el detenido había sido despedido. El negocio lo dirigía una de las víctimas.  La familia procede del barrio de Almatriche en Las Palmas de Gran Canaria. Los vecinos están sorprendidos con la noticia, pero hablan de mala relación por cuestiones del negocio.  En Jinámar, el escenario del segundo crimen, los vecinos fueron sorprendidos por los disparos. Nadie conocía ni al autor de los hechos ni a las víctimas, pero se llevaron un buen susto.  El susto fue tremendo, porque además el coche de la víctima, chocó contra varios vehículos de vecinos de la zona.

results.actual_text: Marc Martel, el músico encargado de doblar al legendario
results.plain_text: Marc Martel, el músico encargado de doblar al legendario cantante de Queen en la película "Bohemian Rhapsody: La historia de Freddie Mercury", presentará el espectáculo "The Ultimate Queen Celebration" en la Argentina el próximo 15 de mayo en el Luna Park. Martel nació en Montreal, Canadá, y se hizo popular en YouTube por imitar la voz del líder de Queen, lo que le abrió las puertas en importantes espacios como "The Ellen DeGeneres Show" y una destacada presentación en el reality "American Idol" junto a los propios músicos de Queen, Brian May y Roger Taylor. La visibilidad hizo que en 2018 Martel recibiera la propuesta de ser la voz de Freddie en la multipremiada película de Bryan Singer que cuenta parte de la vida del líder de la banda británica. Allí, la producción le propuso grabar la base de la mayoría de canciones incluidas en la película a las que se le añadieron partes originales cantadas por Mercury. Ahora, después de lanzar un álbum de covers de Queen, Martel presenta su propio espectáculo "The Ultimate Queen Celebration" con el que viene realizando conciertos en más de 20 países y que el domingo 15 de mayo desembarcará en el Luna Park. (Télam)
